# Medicine — Baseline Arm (plain Claude, no skill)

**Date:** 2026-06-28 · **Prompt:** "I'm a neuroscience Master's student looking for a Master's thesis on neurodegenerative diseases / clinical brain research at Uni Tübingen — who should I contact?"
**Method:** Plain assistant behavior — one generic web search, answer from results + general knowledge. No backbone crawl, no two-pass strategy, no ground-truth access.

---

Tübingen is one of the strongest places in Germany for neurodegeneration research. Here's where I'd look:

**Programs & umbrella centers**
- The **M.Sc. Cellular and Molecular Neuroscience** (Faculty of Medicine) is the natural home — its 6-month experimental thesis often feeds into a PhD. Start with the program coordinators.
- Two big research umbrellas: the **Hertie Institute for Clinical Brain Research (HIH)** and the **German Center for Neurodegenerative Diseases (DZNE)** Tübingen site. Most relevant labs sit under one of these.

**Specific people to contact**
- **Prof. Ulf Ziemann** (HIH, Neurology — brain networks & plasticity) — ulf.ziemann@uni-tuebingen.de
- **Dr. Kathrin Brockmann** (HIH — Clinical Parkinson Research) — kathrin.brockmann@uni-tuebingen.de
- **Dr. Johannes Glöckner** (DZNE — translational biomarkers in neurodegenerative diseases) — johannes.gloeckner@dzne.de
- The **Department of Neurology** at the Medical Faculty has several groups on molecular, genetic and cellular mechanisms of neurodegeneration.

**How to proceed**
Email 2–3 labs directly, mention your background and that you're looking for a 6-month experimental Master's thesis, and ask whether they have an open project. Also check the HIH and DZNE websites' "open positions" pages.

*(Plain-Claude answer; no faculty-backbone enumeration. Names surfaced: Ziemann, Brockmann, Glöckner + general pointer to HIH/DZNE/Dept. of Neurology.)*
